sections.name: Dulac
sections.text: *Nethys Note: No description has been provided for this creature.*

link.name: Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death pg. 9
link.alt: Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death pg. 9

link.name: Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death pg. 9
link.alt: Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death pg. 9

traits.name: Unique
traits.text: A rules element with this trait is one-of-a-kind. The DC of Recall Knowledge checks related to creatures with this trait is increased by 10.

traits.name: Caligni
traits.text: These subterranean people have darkvision, and some have powers to create darkness.

traits.name: Humanoid
traits.text: Humanoid creatures reason and act much like humans. They typically stand upright and have two arms and two legs.

link.name: Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death pg. 9
link.alt: Pathfinder #165: Eyes of Empty Death pg. 9

automatic_abilities.name: Death Shadows

traits.name: Cold
traits.text: Effects with this trait deal cold damage. Creatures with this trait have a connection to magical cold.

traits.name: Darkness
traits.text: Darkness effects extinguish non-magical light in the area, and can counteract less powerful magical light. You must usually target light magic with your darkness magic directly to counteract the light, but some darkness spells automatically attempt to counteract light.

automatic_abilities.text: When Dulac dies, her body is destroyed in a burst of chilling shadows. All creatures in a 20-foot burst take 8d8 cold damage (DC 28 basic Reflex save). Dulac's gear and treasure are unaffected by the shadows and are left in a pile where she died.

automatic_abilities.name: Opportune Step
automatic_abilities.trigger: A dazzled or blinded creature, or a creature with light blindness, ends its move adjacent to Dulac

automatic_abilities.name: Warding Shove
automatic_abilities.trigger: An ally within Dulac's reach is targeted with a ranged or melee attack by an attacker she can see
automatic_abilities.effect: The ally gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack, and the ally can Step after the attack.

traits.name: Disarm
traits.text: You can use this weapon to Disarm with the Athletics skill even if you don't have a free hand. This uses the weapon's reach (if different from your own) and adds the weapon's item bonus to attack rolls (if any) as an item bonus to the Athletics check. If you critically fail a check to Disarm using the weapon, you can drop the weapon to take the effects of a failure instead of a critical failure. On a critical success, you still need a free hand if you want to take the item.

traits.name: Sweep
traits.text: This weapon makes wide sweeping or spinning attacks, making it easier to attack multiple enemies. When you attack with this weapon, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your attack roll if you already attempted to attack a different target this turn using this weapon.

traits.name: Trip
traits.text: You can use this weapon to Trip with the Athletics skill even if you don't have a free hand. This uses the weapon's reach (if different from your own) and adds the weapon's item bonus to attack rolls as an item bonus to the Athletics check. If you critically fail a check to Trip using the weapon, you can drop the weapon to take the effects of a failure instead of a critical failure.

ability.name: Sneak Attack
ability.text: Dulac deals an additional 1d6 precision damage to flat-footed creatures.

universal_monster_ability.name: Sneak Attack
universal_monster_ability.text: When the monster Strikes a creature that has the flat-footed condition with an agile or finesse melee weapon, an agile or finesse unarmed attack, or a ranged weapon attack, it also deals the listed precision damage. For a ranged attack with a thrown weapon, that weapon must also be an agile or finesse weapon.
